hypothesis: If an XGBoost ∆-learning model is trained to predict the correction from GFN1-xTB to DFT (PBE/HSE06) band gaps using ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptors, then its cross-validated MAE on MOF band gaps will be lower than both direct XGBoost models and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN baselines because ∆-learning exploits systematic error correlations between semi-empirical and DFT calculations.
